title: A Theory of Fun for Game Design
creator: Raph Koster
description: An illustrated argument that much of the pleasure of games comes from recognizing and mastering patterns. The second edition adds material while preserving the short, approachable structure of the original.
useful: A readable starting point for questioning what players learn from rules, although its central theory is best compared with other perspectives.
audience: New designers and general readers looking for an accessible argument about why play can be pleasurable.
learning: Relate play to pattern recognition and learning
Distinguish surface fiction from underlying challenge
Critique a broad theory using examples of your own
